name: device_technology_emergence
description: Measures the maturity and material class of the power semiconductor devices utilized in the research.
low: Relies exclusively on mature silicon-based devices such as standard IGBTs or Si MOSFETs.
high: Focuses on wide-bandgap or emerging material devices such as SiC, GaN, Ga2O3, or diamond semiconductors.

name: experimental_validation_depth
description: Indicates the extent of physical hardware verification versus theoretical or simulation-only analysis.
low: Purely analytical derivation or simulation-based study without hardware implementation.
high: Comprehensive hardware prototype validation including measured waveforms, efficiency curves, and dynamic response data.

name: power_rating_scale
description: Characterizes the magnitude of power processing handled by the proposed system or component.
low: Micro-watt to watt-level power handling typical of IoT, RF, or sensor node applications.
high: Kilowatt to megawatt-scale power handling typical of industrial drives, traction, or grid infrastructure.

name: switching_frequency_regime
description: Denotes the operational switching frequency range of the power converter topology.
low: Operation at mains frequency (50/60 Hz) or low kilohertz ranges.
high: High-frequency operation in the megahertz range enabling high power density or RF applications.

name: control_strategy_complexity
description: Assesses the sophistication of the control algorithms employed for regulation and modulation.
low: Linear control techniques such as standard PI/PD loops or basic hysteresis control.
high: Advanced non-linear, model-predictive, sliding-mode, adaptive, or data-driven/AI-based control algorithms.

name: system_abstraction_level
description: Describes the hierarchical layer of the power electronics stack addressed by the research.
low: Device-level focus including gate-driver design, parasitic extraction, or module packaging/thermal analysis.
high: System-level focus including grid integration, microgrid dispatch, or fleet-level energy management.

name: topology_structural_complexity
description: Evaluates the architectural intricacy of the power converter circuit topology.
low: Basic hard-switching two-level topologies such as Buck, Boost, or Flyback converters.
high: Complex multi-level, resonant, matrix, solid-state transformer, or multi-port hybrid architectures.

name: loss_analysis_granularity
description: Measures the depth of quantitative analysis regarding power losses and thermal performance.
low: Efficiency reported as a single aggregate metric or loss mechanisms are not explicitly analyzed.
high: Detailed quantitative loss breakdown (conduction/switching), thermal modeling, or explicit soft-switching mechanism verification.

name: implementation_digitalization
description: Indicates whether the solution emphasizes analog hardware implementation or digital algorithmic execution.
low: Analog circuit implementation, discrete component design, or hardware-centric focus.
high: Digital implementation via FPGA/DSP, algorithm-centric development, or digital twin simulation focus.

name: application_domain_narrowness
description: Reflects how tightly the research is constrained to a specific application versus general-purpose utility.
low: General-purpose converter theory or topology with broad applicability across multiple domains.
high: Highly specific application constraints such as aerospace propulsion, medical MRI power supplies, or specific EV traction standards.
